semanticCore: Fragment, piece, eclipse, gloom, segment, or sheet
quranicRelevance: Pieces/fragments of sky falling (Kisafan - 'wa in yaraw kisafan mina as-samai', 'aw tusqita as-samai... kisafan').

keyDerivations.meaning: Fragment, piece, segment, sheet, or chunk (of sky)

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَوْ تُسْقِطَ السَّمَاءَ كَمَا زَعَمْتَ عَلَيْنَا كِسَفًا (17:92): Or you cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces/fragments (pagan challenge to the Prophet).
discardedNoise: Solar eclipse (Kusoof) in medieval astronomical charts.
State of sorrow and depression (Kaseef) in popular poetry.
summaryEn: The root represents pieces and fragments (Kisaf), documenting the pagan demand for sky-fragments to fall as proof, and their denial even if they saw it falling.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر القطع والكسف الساقطة، واصفاً تحدي قريش بنزول قطع من السماء عذاباً (كسفاً)، وعنادهم بادعاء القطع الساقطة سحاباً متراكماً.
